[DOC1_A9 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > CCS CONCEPTS]
## CCS CONCEPTS

[DOC1_A83 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.3 LambdaLoss, RankNet and LambdaRank. We used NDCGLoss2++ of [34], formulated as follows:

[DOC1_A84 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$l(s, y)=-\sum_{y_{i}>y_{j}} \log _{2} \sum_{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sigma\left(s_{i}-s_{j}\right)}}\right)^{\left(\rho_{i j}+\mu \delta_{i j}\right)\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right|} H(\pi \mid s)$

[DOC1_A85 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A86 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\begin{gathered}
G_{i}=\frac{2^{y_{i}}-1}{\operatorname{maxDCG}} \\
\rho_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{i}}-\frac{1}{D_{j}}\right| \\
\delta_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}}-\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}+1}\right| \\
D_{i}=\log _{2}(1+i)
\end{gathered}
$$

[DOC1_A87 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
and $H(\pi \mid s)$ is a hard assignment distribution of permutations, i.e.

[DOC1_A88 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
H(\hat{\pi} \mid s)=1 \text { and } H(\pi \mid s)=0 \text { for all } \pi \neq \hat{\pi}
$$

[DOC1_A89 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where $\hat{\pi}$ is the permutation in which all items are sorted by decreasing scores $\boldsymbol{s}$. Fixed parameter $\mu$ is set to 10.0 .

[DOC1_A90 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
By removing the exponent in $l(s, y)$ formula we obtain the RankNet loss function, weighing each score pair identically. Similarly, we may obtain differently weighted RankNet variants by changing the formula in the exponent.

[DOC1_A91 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
To obtain a LambdaRank formula, replace the exponent with

[DOC1_A92 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\Delta \operatorname{NDCG}(i, j)=\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right| \rho_{i j}
$$

[DOC1_A93 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.4 ListNet and ListMLE. ListNet loss [9] is given by the following formula:

[DOC1_A94 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(s, y)=-\sum_{j} \operatorname{softmax}(\boldsymbol{y})_{j} \times \log \left(\operatorname{softmax}(s)_{j}\right)
$$

[DOC1_A95 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
In binary version, softmax of ground truth $\boldsymbol{y}$ is omitted for singleclick lists and replaced with normalisation by the number of clicks for multiple-click lists.

[DOC1_A96 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
ListMLE [35] is given by:

[DOC1_A97 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(\boldsymbol{s}, \boldsymbol{y})=-\log P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})
$$

[DOC1_A98 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A99 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})=\prod_{i}^{n} \frac{\exp \left(f\left(x_{y(i)}\right)\right)}{\sum_{k=i}^{n} \exp \left(f\left(x_{y(k)}\right)\right)}
$$

Bruce Croft. 2017. Neural Ranking Mode...", "text": "[DOC1_A9 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > CCS CONCEPTS]
## CCS CONCEPTS

[DOC1_A83 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.3 LambdaLoss, RankNet and LambdaRank. We used NDCGLoss2++ of [34], formulated as follows:

[DOC1_A84 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$l(s, y)=-\sum_{y_{i}>y_{j}} \log _{2} \sum_{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sigma\left(s_{i}-s_{j}\right)}}\right)^{\left(\rho_{i j}+\mu \delta_{i j}\right)\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right|} H(\pi \mid s)$

[DOC1_A85 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A86 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\begin{gathered}
G_{i}=\frac{2^{y_{i}}-1}{\operatorname{maxDCG}} \\
\rho_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{i}}-\frac{1}{D_{j}}\right| \\
\delta_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}}-\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}+1}\right| \\
D_{i}=\log _{2}(1+i)
\end{gathered}
$$

[DOC1_A87 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
and $H(\pi \mid s)$ is a hard assignment distribution of permutations, i.e.

[DOC1_A88 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
H(\hat{\pi} \mid s)=1 \text { and } H(\pi \mid s)=0 \text { for all } \pi \neq \hat{\pi}
$$

[DOC1_A89 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where $\hat{\pi}$ is the permutation in which all items are sorted by decreasing scores $\boldsymbol{s}$. Fixed parameter $\mu$ is set to 10.0 .

[DOC1_A90 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
By removing the exponent in $l(s, y)$ formula we obtain the RankNet loss function, weighing each score pair identically. Similarly, we may obtain differently weighted RankNet variants by changing the formula in the exponent.

[DOC1_A91 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
To obtain a LambdaRank formula, replace the exponent with

[DOC1_A92 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\Delta \operatorname{NDCG}(i, j)=\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right| \rho_{i j}
$$

[DOC1_A93 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.4 ListNet and ListMLE. ListNet loss [9] is given by the following formula:

[DOC1_A94 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(s, y)=-\sum_{j} \operatorname{softmax}(\boldsymbol{y})_{j} \times \log \left(\operatorname{softmax}(s)_{j}\right)
$$

[DOC1_A95 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
In binary version, softmax of ground truth $\boldsymbol{y}$ is omitted for singleclick lists and replaced with normalisation by the number of clicks for multiple-click lists.

[DOC1_A96 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
ListMLE [35] is given by:

[DOC1_A97 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(\boldsymbol{s}, \boldsymbol{y})=-\log P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})
$$

[DOC1_A98 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A99 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})=\prod_{i}^{n} \frac{\exp \left(f\left(x_{y(i)}\right)\right)}{\sum_{k=i}^{n} \exp \left(f\left(x_{y(k)}\right)\right)}
$$

Bruce Croft. 2017. Neural Ranking Mode...", "text": "[DOC1_A9 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > CCS CONCEPTS]
## CCS CONCEPTS

[DOC1_A83 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.3 LambdaLoss, RankNet and LambdaRank. We used NDCGLoss2++ of [34], formulated as follows:

[DOC1_A84 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$l(s, y)=-\sum_{y_{i}>y_{j}} \log _{2} \sum_{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sigma\left(s_{i}-s_{j}\right)}}\right)^{\left(\rho_{i j}+\mu \delta_{i j}\right)\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right|} H(\pi \mid s)$

[DOC1_A85 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A86 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\begin{gathered}
G_{i}=\frac{2^{y_{i}}-1}{\operatorname{maxDCG}} \\
\rho_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{i}}-\frac{1}{D_{j}}\right| \\
\delta_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}}-\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}+1}\right| \\
D_{i}=\log _{2}(1+i)
\end{gathered}
$$

[DOC1_A87 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
and $H(\pi \mid s)$ is a hard assignment distribution of permutations, i.e.

[DOC1_A88 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
H(\hat{\pi} \mid s)=1 \text { and } H(\pi \mid s)=0 \text { for all } \pi \neq \hat{\pi}
$$

[DOC1_A89 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where $\hat{\pi}$ is the permutation in which all items are sorted by decreasing scores $\boldsymbol{s}$. Fixed parameter $\mu$ is set to 10.0 .

[DOC1_A90 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
By removing the exponent in $l(s, y)$ formula we obtain the RankNet loss function, weighing each score pair identically. Similarly, we may obtain differently weighted RankNet variants by changing the formula in the exponent.

[DOC1_A91 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
To obtain a LambdaRank formula, replace the exponent with

[DOC1_A92 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\Delta \operatorname{NDCG}(i, j)=\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right| \rho_{i j}
$$

[DOC1_A93 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.4 ListNet and ListMLE. ListNet loss [9] is given by the following formula:

[DOC1_A94 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(s, y)=-\sum_{j} \operatorname{softmax}(\boldsymbol{y})_{j} \times \log \left(\operatorname{softmax}(s)_{j}\right)
$$

[DOC1_A95 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
In binary version, softmax of ground truth $\boldsymbol{y}$ is omitted for singleclick lists and replaced with normalisation by the number of clicks for multiple-click lists.

[DOC1_A96 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
ListMLE [35] is given by:

[DOC1_A97 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(\boldsymbol{s}, \boldsymbol{y})=-\log P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})
$$

[DOC1_A98 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A99 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})=\prod_{i}^{n} \frac{\exp \left(f\left(x_{y(i)}\right)\right)}{\sum_{k=i}^{n} \exp \left(f\left(x_{y(k)}\right)\right)}
$$

Dally. 2015. Learning Both Weights and Connections for Effic...", "text": "[DOC1_A9 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > CCS CONCEPTS]
## CCS CONCEPTS

[DOC1_A83 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.3 LambdaLoss, RankNet and LambdaRank. We used NDCGLoss2++ of [34], formulated as follows:

[DOC1_A84 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$l(s, y)=-\sum_{y_{i}>y_{j}} \log _{2} \sum_{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sigma\left(s_{i}-s_{j}\right)}}\right)^{\left(\rho_{i j}+\mu \delta_{i j}\right)\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right|} H(\pi \mid s)$

[DOC1_A85 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A86 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\begin{gathered}
G_{i}=\frac{2^{y_{i}}-1}{\operatorname{maxDCG}} \\
\rho_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{i}}-\frac{1}{D_{j}}\right| \\
\delta_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}}-\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}+1}\right| \\
D_{i}=\log _{2}(1+i)
\end{gathered}
$$

[DOC1_A87 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
and $H(\pi \mid s)$ is a hard assignment distribution of permutations, i.e.

[DOC1_A88 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
H(\hat{\pi} \mid s)=1 \text { and } H(\pi \mid s)=0 \text { for all } \pi \neq \hat{\pi}
$$

[DOC1_A89 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where $\hat{\pi}$ is the permutation in which all items are sorted by decreasing scores $\boldsymbol{s}$. Fixed parameter $\mu$ is set to 10.0 .

[DOC1_A90 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
By removing the exponent in $l(s, y)$ formula we obtain the RankNet loss function, weighing each score pair identically. Similarly, we may obtain differently weighted RankNet variants by changing the formula in the exponent.

[DOC1_A91 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
To obtain a LambdaRank formula, replace the exponent with

[DOC1_A92 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\Delta \operatorname{NDCG}(i, j)=\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right| \rho_{i j}
$$

[DOC1_A93 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.4 ListNet and ListMLE. ListNet loss [9] is given by the following formula:

[DOC1_A94 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(s, y)=-\sum_{j} \operatorname{softmax}(\boldsymbol{y})_{j} \times \log \left(\operatorname{softmax}(s)_{j}\right)
$$

[DOC1_A95 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
In binary version, softmax of ground truth $\boldsymbol{y}$ is omitted for singleclick lists and replaced with normalisation by the number of clicks for multiple-click lists.

[DOC1_A96 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
ListMLE [35] is given by:

[DOC1_A97 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(\boldsymbol{s}, \boldsymbol{y})=-\log P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})
$$

[DOC1_A98 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A99 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})=\prod_{i}^{n} \frac{\exp \left(f\left(x_{y(i)}\right)\right)}{\sum_{k=i}^{n} \exp \left(f\left(x_{y(k)}\right)\right)}
$$

## CCS CONCEPTS

[DOC1_A83 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.3 LambdaLoss, RankNet and LambdaRank. We used NDCGLoss2++ of [34], formulated as follows:

[DOC1_A84 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$l(s, y)=-\sum_{y_{i}>y_{j}} \log _{2} \sum_{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sigma\left(s_{i}-s_{j}\right)}}\right)^{\left(\rho_{i j}+\mu \delta_{i j}\right)\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right|} H(\pi \mid s)$

[DOC1_A85 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A86 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\begin{gathered}
G_{i}=\frac{2^{y_{i}}-1}{\operatorname{maxDCG}} \\
\rho_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{i}}-\frac{1}{D_{j}}\right| \\
\delta_{i j}=\left|\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}}-\frac{1}{D_{|i-j|}+1}\right| \\
D_{i}=\log _{2}(1+i)
\end{gathered}
$$

[DOC1_A87 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
and $H(\pi \mid s)$ is a hard assignment distribution of permutations, i.e.

[DOC1_A88 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
H(\hat{\pi} \mid s)=1 \text { and } H(\pi \mid s)=0 \text { for all } \pi \neq \hat{\pi}
$$

[DOC1_A89 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where $\hat{\pi}$ is the permutation in which all items are sorted by decreasing scores $\boldsymbol{s}$. Fixed parameter $\mu$ is set to 10.0 .

[DOC1_A90 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
By removing the exponent in $l(s, y)$ formula we obtain the RankNet loss function, weighing each score pair identically. Similarly, we may obtain differently weighted RankNet variants by changing the formula in the exponent.

[DOC1_A91 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
To obtain a LambdaRank formula, replace the exponent with

[DOC1_A92 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
\Delta \operatorname{NDCG}(i, j)=\left|G_{i}-G_{j}\right| \rho_{i j}
$$

[DOC1_A93 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
5.2.4 ListNet and ListMLE. ListNet loss [9] is given by the following formula:

[DOC1_A94 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(s, y)=-\sum_{j} \operatorname{softmax}(\boldsymbol{y})_{j} \times \log \left(\operatorname{softmax}(s)_{j}\right)
$$

[DOC1_A95 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
In binary version, softmax of ground truth $\boldsymbol{y}$ is omitted for singleclick lists and replaced with normalisation by the number of clicks for multiple-click lists.

[DOC1_A96 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
ListMLE [35] is given by:

[DOC1_A97 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
l(\boldsymbol{s}, \boldsymbol{y})=-\log P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})
$$

[DOC1_A98 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
where

[DOC1_A99 | Context-Aware Learning to Rank with Self-Attention > 5 EXPERIMENTS > 5.2 Loss Functions]
$$
P(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{s})=\prod_{i}^{n} \frac{\exp \left(f\left(x_{y(i)}\right)\right)}{\sum_{k=i}^{n} \exp \left(f\left(x_{y(k)}\right)\right)}
$$
